description: In shell, run the package-local gate, fix the first owned diagnostic, rerun, then broaden.
body: In shell, the fastest path through errors is to let bash -n / sh -n syntax checks before execution and shellcheck warnings (especially SC2086 word-splitting and SC2046) drive one fix at a time rather than guessing. Use when the script under its declared shell (#!/usr/bin/env bash vs POSIX sh) or the analyzers report multiple diagnostics. Do not batch speculative edits across files, and avoid suppressing diagnostics to move on. Actions: inspect the package, run the gate, fix only the first line from bash -x trace or shellcheck's SC code, rerun bats tests or a guarded dry-run with set -euo pipefail, then widen to the script under its declared shell (#!/usr/bin/env bash vs POSIX sh) for the whole package. Validation signals include a shrinking diagnostic count, a passing focused test, and a clean package-wide gate. Risk boundary: if a diagnostic implies a design or API change, stop and escalate instead of forcing the gate green.
